topPlaces.name: Cheliyama
topPlaces.description: Cheliyama (also referred to as Cheyama) is a village in the Raghunathpur II CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Usir
topPlaces.description: Usir is a village in the Raghunathpur II CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Dubra, Purulia
topPlaces.description: Dubra is a census town and a gram panchayat in the Para CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Telkupi
topPlaces.description: Telkupi (or Tailakampi) is a submerged location of archaeological interest in Purulia district of West Bengal, India. The area, along with most of the temples situated there, was submerged in 1959 as a consequence to the construction of a dam across Damodar river at Panchet in Dhanbad district, then in Bihar.

topPlaces.name: Achkoda
topPlaces.description: Achkoda (sometimes spelled Anchkoda) is a village in the Neturia CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Kanki, Purulia
topPlaces.description: Kanki is a census town in the Para CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Santaldih
topPlaces.description: Santaldih is a census town in the Raghunathpur II CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Nabagram, Purulia
topPlaces.description: Nabagram is a census town in the Raghunathpur II CD block in the Raghunathpur subdivision of the Purulia district in West Bengal, India.

Telkupi (or Tailakampi) is a submerged location of archaeological interest in Purulia district of West Bengal, India. The area, along with most of the temples situated there, was submerged in 1959 as a consequence to the construction of a dam across Damodar river at Panchet in Dhanbad district, then in Bihar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Temple of Banda?
faq.a: The best time to visit Temple of Banda is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Temple of Banda?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (131 km) and the nearest railway station is Dhanbad Junction (26 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Temple of Banda?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Temple of Banda at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Temple of Banda?
faq.a: Stays in Temple of Banda start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Temple of Banda?
faq.a: The top attractions are Cheliyama, Usir, Dubra, Purulia, Telkupi, Achkoda.
